title: Verification Report
description: Reliability Harness v2 — Verification Report. Produced by the clean-checkout verifier on a fresh worktree. Machine-enforced: a failed check CANNOT be overridden by an LLM verdict. STATUS is one of VERIFIED | PARTIALLY_VERIFIED | BLOCKED | UNVERIFIED (no percentages, no 'ship with 85%').

status.description: VERIFIED only if every done-gate passes. PARTIALLY_VERIFIED if non-critical gaps remain. BLOCKED if a blocking_unknown is unresolved or a CRITICAL/HIGH finding is open. UNVERIFIED if evidence is insufficient.

worktree.description: Identifies the clean checkout used for verification.

patch_applied.description: Patch identifier (e.g., commit SHA, patch file hash).

fresh_checkout.description: Must be true. If false or absent the schema rejects the report, so VERIFIED is unreachable without proof of a clean checkout. Verification on a dirty/shared worktree is invalid.

evidence.description: Every command actually executed during verification, with its observed result. No claim may rest on an unexecuted command.

command.description: The exact command executed (e.g., 'npm test', 'cargo build', 'tsc --noEmit').

summary.description: What the command printed or did. Quotes / trimmed output encouraged.

passed.description: True iff the command produced the expected success result. False otherwise.

acceptance_results.description: One entry per acceptance criterion in the task contract. Every criterion must be addressed.

satisfied.description: True iff concrete evidence demonstrates the condition holds. False if unsupported.

evidence_ref.description: Pointer to the evidence[] entry (or entries) backing this result.

scope_violations.description: Files modified outside task-contract.allowed_scope. Any non-empty list forces STATUS != VERIFIED.

residual_unknowns.description: Unknowns that remain after verification. Surface explicitly — never paper over.

severity_findings.description: Open CRITICAL/HIGH findings from the adversary or verifier. Any open CRITICAL/HIGH forces STATUS != VERIFIED.

preexisting_vs_introduced.description: Distinguishes failures present at base_commit from failures introduced by this change. Required to claim VERIFIED.

preexisting_failures.description: Failures present at base_commit (documented in baseline).

introduced_failures.description: Failures introduced by the patch. Must be empty for VERIFIED.
